title: Ways of Seeing
author: John Berger
description: A groundbreaking work on how we perceive art, exploring the relationship between seeing and knowing. Berger challenges conventional approaches to visual culture with essays that remain as vital today as when first published.

title: Nervous Conditions
author: Tsitsi Dangarembga
description: A powerful novel set in colonial Rhodesia, exploring the tensions between tradition and modernity through the eyes of a young girl determined to get an education. A cornerstone of African literature.

title: Entangled Life
author: Merlin Sheldrake
description: A mind-expanding exploration of the hidden world of fungi — the organisms that sustain life, shape our minds, and hold the key to understanding our planet.

title: Black Skin, White Masks
author: Frantz Fanon
description: A radical analysis of the psychology of racism and colonialism, examining how the colonised mind internalises the values of the oppressor. Essential, urgent, enduring.

title: Braiding Sweetgrass
author: Robin Wall Kimmerer
description: Indigenous wisdom, scientific knowledge, and the teachings of plants woven together in a luminous meditation on reciprocity between humans and the living world.

title: Ways of Curating
author: Hans Ulrich Obrist
description: A personal history of curating by one of the art world's most influential figures. Obrist traces connections between exhibitions, conversations, and the act of bringing ideas together.
